tools.name: get_skills
tools.description: Lists main skill ids (Notes, Mindmap, Settings, …). Heavy analytics/statistics tooling is omitted here—use get_analytics_skills then inject_skill. Does not enable module tools by itself.

tools.name: fetch_skill
tools.description: Read-only preview: returns the system prompt fragment and tool schemas for one skill_id. Does NOT register those tools for this turn — you cannot call list_notes, read_mindmap, etc. from fetch_skill alone. To actually run module tools, call inject_skill with that skill_id. Hidden-catalog ids (e.g. Analytics) come from get_analytics_skills.

skill_id.description: Skill id from get_skills, get_analytics_skills, or NONE

tools.name: inject_skill
tools.description: REQUIRED to use a module's tools (e.g. list_notes for Notes). Statistics/analytics tools are discovered via get_analytics_skills, then inject_skill with that skill id (e.g. Analytics). fetch_skill previews without enabling. navigate_to does not load tools.

skill_id.description: Target skill id from get_skills or get_analytics_skills; NONE clears override.

apply_for_conversation.description: Default true: keep this skill for the rest of this chat thread when a thread key exists

tools.name: get_analytics_skills
tools.description: Lists skills omitted from get_skills (many stats_* tools—hard for small models). Does not enable them; call inject_skill with the returned skill id (typically Analytics).

tools.name: navigate_to
tools.description: Opens a module screen in the app (overview, notes, chat, mindmap, path, settings). UI navigation only — does NOT load skill tools. You still cannot call list_notes until you inject_skill with skill_id Notes.
